codeReview.summary: Solid implementation of vertical scrolling across drawing library and compositor. The core algorithms (byte_to_visual_line, scroll_for_cursor, draw_tt_sel_scroll) are correct and handle edge cases (empty text, soft wrap, cursor at boundaries). Compositor integration is clean: scroll offset tracked as a static, auto-updated after every editor message batch, and saved/restored across context switches. Full surface clear per frame is the correct trade-off for scroll. All 13 new tests pass and cover the main code paths. Two tests are trivially weak (testing arithmetic identity rather than actual behavior), but the interactive visual verification compensates. Code duplication between draw_tt_sel and draw_tt_sel_scroll is a minor style debt.

issues.description: scroll_offset_preserved_across_context_switch test is trivially weak — it saves a u32 to a variable and asserts it equals itself (assert_eq!(7, 7)). It does not test the compositor's actual SAVED_EDITOR_SCROLL mechanism. The test name implies integration-level verification but only tests integer assignment.

scroll_clips_lines_above_and_below test only checks byte_to_visual_line returns the correct line number — it does not verify that pixels above the scroll offset are actually absent from the framebuffer or that pixels below max_y are clipped. The final comment says 'We can verify: the draw function should place line 1 at origin_y + (1-1)*line_height' but never asserts it. A stronger test would call draw_tt_sel_scroll on a small surface and check that specific pixel rows are blank.

draw_tt_sel_scroll largely duplicates draw_tt_sel (~80 lines of shared logic). The non-scroll version could delegate to draw_tt_sel_scroll with scroll_offset=0, eliminating the duplication. Low priority since the code is correct and the methods are side-by-side.

Missing blank line between total_visual_lines() and scroll_for_cursor() — the closing brace of total_visual_lines immediately precedes the doc comment of scroll_for_cursor. Inconsistent with the rest of the file's style where functions are separated by blank lines.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Two tests (scroll_offset_preserved_across_context_switch, scroll_clips_lines_above_and_below) are trivially weak — they test arithmetic identity or line numbering rather than the actual behavior described in their names. AGENTS.md has no guidance on minimum test quality or what constitutes a meaningful integration vs unit test for compositor state.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: system/test/tests/drawing.rs:4272 — test body is 'let x = 7; assert_eq!(x, 7)' dressed up as a context switch test. system/test/tests/drawing.rs:4249 — test claims to verify clipping but only checks byte_to_visual_line.

summary: Scrolling feature passes review. Implementation is correct, well-structured, and covers all requirements: scroll offset tracking, auto-scroll on cursor movement, content clipping, Home/End support, selection survival, and context switch preservation. The visual verification was thorough (6 interactive checks including Home/End/selection/context switch). Two non-blocking issues: weak tests that verify arithmetic rather than behavior, and code duplication between draw_tt_sel and draw_tt_sel_scroll. No blocking issues found.
